arts.sum: Barcelona ended 2024 in very poor form, so they will have been keen to start 2025 on the front foot. As things stand, they are well on course to do so having doubled their lead over Barbastro in their Copa del Rey round of 16 tie. arts.ttl: Olmo in limbo as La Liga and Spanish FA rule against Barca
arts.sum: Barcelona midfielder Dani Olmo and forward Pau Victor have been blocked from playing in the rest of the 2024-25 season by Spain's football authorities.

After it was reported in the last couple of days that Girona were looking to sign Eric Garcia, a second Barcelona player has now appear on their radar. On Friday, Barcelona made their long-awaited return to La Liga’s 1:1 rule, and on the back of this, they could sanction the sale of Eric Garcia in […] The post Girona eye double Barcelona deal appeared first on Football España.

arts.ttl: Barcelona plot final attempt to re-register Dani Olmo after official La Liga ruling
arts.sum: Barcelona's plea to re-register Dani Olmo was officially rejected but the club remain "optimistic" a resolution will be found.

arts.ttl: Barcelona uncertainty goes on as latest request to register Dani Olmo is rejected
arts.sum: La Liga and RFEF reject Olmo and Víctor registrations Barcelona likely to take case to Spain’s sports council Barcelona have failed in their latest attempt to register the Spain international Dani Olmo for the second half of the season, but are likely to take their case to Spain’s sports council as they continue to challenge the decision. On Saturday, La Liga and the Spanish football federation (RFEF) jointly rejected the registrations of Olmo and his teammate Pau Víctor, but the wording of their ruling may open the door for the Catalan club to challenge the rule that prevents them from including the two players in their squad.
